title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Boris Anchukov leaves the room, George and Chief Balthazar discuss the servant's volatile behavior, with George questioning whether Anchukov's intense grief is genuine devotion or a performance.

Later, Virginia Rue and Anthony Ked walk toward a lake. Anthony recounts his experience handling a body and notes that while Inspector Bates believes he is innocent due to a lack of motive, he remains cautious. Anthony proposes a theory that the victim, identified as Count Stanislaus, might actually be Prince Michael of Hesse-Darmstadt, suggesting the victim may have been an imposter. He asks Virginia to use her influence with the Earl of Carrington to view the body and confirm this. Virginia observes that the estimated time of death, 11:45, aligns with a mysterious note, and she mentions hearing gunshots the previous night, despite Inspector Bartlett's claim that no one else heard them.

To avoid being overheard, the pair rows out onto the lake. Anthony reveals his true identity to Virginia. In response, Virginia admits that her cousin, George Roxborough, had previously attempted to manipulate her into seducing a man named Jimmy McGregor to obtain a memoir. Anthony explains that he knew Virginia was not the author of certain blackmail letters because her psychological reaction proved she would not succumb to such pressure. The conversation concludes with Anthony revealing that one of the letters was mailed from Chimney House, leading both to suspect that the writer used Virginia's name intentionally.
